name: born-to-run
description: Christopher McDougall's journey into the world of the Tarahumara, barefoot running, ultra-endurance, and the discovery that humans are born to run — not fast, but far.
useCase: Improving running form, transitioning to minimalist running, building ultra-endurance, finding joy in running, understanding human evolution as distance runners.
knownRisks: Switching to minimalist or barefoot running too quickly can cause injury — transition slowly over months
The 'shoe as villain' thesis is supported by the Tarahumara evidence but disputed by some sports medicine professionals
Not everyone can or should run barefoot — start on soft surfaces and build gradually
Some of the claims about running form and injury are based on observational rather than controlled studies
